what_was_built: reports/crypto_d1_data_contract_v1/data_contract.md (human-readable: BTC/ETH/SOL spot daily 24/7; required 9-column + optional 7-column OHLCV schema; UTC + 24/7 + missing-day + duplicate + OHLCV-self-consistency + fee/slippage rules; data freeze requirements; no-profit-claim policy)
tests/test_crypto_d1_data_contract.py (28 tests covering schema + safety + validator failure modes + tool stdlib-only + integration with prior bundles)
tools/strategy_candidate_registry.py: crypto_d1_protocol seed extended via extra_files to include the new data_contract docs (lane stays WATCH; never ACTIVE / never STRONG)

distinction_phrases_required_in_md: Crypto trend ideas are not profitable until tested
A good historical chart does not imply future returns
No backtest is authorized by this contract alone
Clean OHLCV data does not imply profit

tests_run.command: python -m pytest tests/test_crypto_d1_data_contract.py tests/test_crypto_d1_protocol.py tests/test_arbitrage_readiness_gate.py tests/test_arbitrage_sample_dataset_plan.py tests/test_arbitrage_data_source_evaluation.py tests/test_arbitrage_qa_harness_spec.py tests/test_arbitrage_dataset_manifest.py tests/test_arbitrage_data_contract.py tests/test_arbitrage_research_protocol.py tests/test_strategy_candidate_registry.py tests/test_strategy_next_bundle.py --rootdir=tests -q

candidate_registry_status_for_crypto_d1_.lane_status_override_active: WATCH (fires because the seed's extra_files now include both protocol.md/.json and data_contract.md/.json, all of which exist on disk)
guardrails_held: never STRONG evidence in the auto-build path

next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.title: Arbitrage research protocol
next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.note: Both arbitrage and crypto_d1 are now WATCH (same +15 bonus). Arbitrage still wins via the existing protocol-first / data-first scoring hints in its queue item. Deterministic logic NOT artificially modified. Operator should consult both lanes' next-step lists to pick the actual Bundle 13.

how_this_follows_protocol: Crypto-D1 Protocol Memo v1 (Bundle 11) declared a P1_data_contract phase as a required future artifact.
This bundle authors that data contract -- it is the spec only; no data is fetched, no backtest is run, no dataset is processed.
All seven safety flags from the protocol are carried verbatim into the data contract (research_only=true, six pinned-False execution/fetch/connection/backtest/dataset-processing flags).
Lane scope is identical to the protocol: BTC/ETH/SOL, spot only, daily only, 24/7, perps and intraday separately-future protocols.

Fee/slippage requirements echo the protocol's fee_slippage_requirements with the addition of contract-specific assertions (taker default, conservative slippage, cost-sensitivity checks before PASS, fees as a distinct PnL line, no-profitability-claim-if-fees-ignored).
Data requirements echo the protocol's data_requirements with concrete column-level schema, validity rules, and minimum-viable-dataset shape.
recommended_next_bundle: Crypto-D1 Dataset Manifest v1 (P2) -- per-dataset manifest schema for the crypto-D1 lane. Spec only.
Crypto-D1 Data QA / Freeze Spec v1 (P2) -- QA harness spec for daily-crypto data. Spec only, no harness run.
Crypto-D1 Baseline Backtest Plan v1 (P3 / P4) -- pre-registration memo for the FIRST candidate strategy family + IS / OOS split. Plan only, no backtest run.
Crypto-D1 Data Acquisition Authorization Draft -- still spec-only; only if the operator wants to start the path toward an actual P2 acquisition. Does not authorize anything by itself.
